prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye be askin' why the vast blue expanse above be colored as 'tis? Listen well, ye landlubber! 'Tis the very breath o' nature's magic, I tell ye! The sunlight, that golden devil's beam, comes crackin' through the heavens like a cannon shot, and when it strikes the air, it scatters like me crew after a rum-soaked night! Them tiny bits o' atmosphere catch the light like a sail catches wind, and blue—bein' the shortest wave—gets tossed about most fiercely. So when ye look up, ye be seein' the sky painted blue as me favorite jacket, scattered by the invisible winds o' light! By Davy Jones' locker, 'tis a marvel that'd make even the saltiest sea dog marvel at the mysteries o' this world! Yarrr!
